title: Merlin
summary: A realtime voice agent that qualifies inbound prospects in the browser, then books the briefing or captures the brief.
situation: Discovery meetings were being spent qualifying callers who could have been qualified up front.
task: Handle an inbound prospect end to end — greet, qualify, book.
Add a mode that returns a structured brief instead of a meeting.
result: Live and embedded on the studio's intake page. Two variants, one binary, rollback-capable deploy.
contributions: Treated the model as untrusted — it can call tools, but it doesn't decide whether the work happened.
A live call ended claiming a brief was sent when none existed. Moved the gate out of the skipped tool into the dispatcher.
Decoupled the caller form from the model, which looped on greetings and called the tool late or never.

keyResults.metricNote: 18 for sales, 8 for intake — one registry, one binary.

items.name: ~400 tests
items.label: Across ~10.5k lines
items.note: Three guards written from real call failures.

items.name: Under 900ms
items.label: Speech-to-audio budget
items.note: Frames forward on arrival; nothing is buffered.

title: ZeroTouch Onboarding
summary: Client onboarding for an Australian accounting firm, built against a hard AML/CTF compliance deadline.
situation: Femia is a $5M accounting practice. New AML/CTF rules meant identifying, verifying and proving every client — all of it by hand.
task: Replace three Excel calculators and paper forms with one system.
Keep pricing private from clients; write every check back to Karbon.
result: Now runs the firm's client onboarding end to end — quote → intake → AML record → write-back into Karbon.
contributions: Split the flow in two: staff see pricing, clients never do. That constraint drove the architecture.
Ruled dry-run and live share one code path — Karbon writes are permanent, with no delete.
Caught that Karbon's number-typed Director ID field silently drops the leading zero from IDs starting 036.

keyResults.metricNote: Quote through AML evidence, running on ZeroTouch.

items.name: 576 rows
items.label: Price matrix, generated not typed
items.note: Built from the firm's own Excel quote builder.

items.name: 269 assertions
items.label: 14 harnesses, no framework
items.note: Pricing is what a client notices being wrong.

title: Narawe
summary: Document intelligence for professional-services firms — scan client PDFs and search them by word or by meaning.
situation: Firms keep client history in scanned paper. Once a document is an image, it is invisible to search.
task: Get paper in with no friction, including from a phone camera.
Make it searchable by exact words and by meaning.
result: A working prototype running scan → OCR → keywords → embedding → hybrid search, plus an auto-written client summary.
contributions: Consolidated three AI providers onto one — Clarifai, OpenRouter and OpenAI became Gemini alone.
Fused keyword and semantic search: people search the exact phrase on an invoice and the vague idea of a letter.
Treated 'the model returned garbage' as a different failure from 'the network failed' — each gets its own retry.

keyResults.metricLabel: Search engines fused into one query
keyResults.metricNote: Full-text and pgvector, fused by reciprocal rank.

items.name: 583 lines
items.label: Markdown to PDF, from scratch
items.note: Rebuilds OCR'd text into a searchable PDF.

items.name: 3 into 1
items.label: AI vendors consolidated
items.note: Two relationships and three failure modes removed.

title: Siratul Mustakim
summary: A voice-first field app that tells a preacher which house to visit next, draws the route, and records the outcome.
situation: Door-to-door outreach loses its memory, and a volunteer walking a street cannot stop to fill in a spreadsheet.
task: Make the loop hands-free: which house, what route, what happened.
Pick by who most needs a visit, not by who is closest.
result: A working prototype with the full voice loop running. Demo mode runs it with no backend at all.
contributions: Cut an always-on server: ephemeral tokens let the browser talk to Gemini while the key stays server-side.
Banned the agent from inventing navigation — a confidently invented street sends someone to the wrong door.
Kept the last house in server context so the agent never echoes an ID back, removing a class of hallucination.

keyResults.metricNote: The preacher never touches the screen.

items.name: 14 / 42 days
items.label: Warm and cooling thresholds
items.note: A no-answer isn't contact, so it never resets the clock.

title: Mofiz Marma
summary: Talk, and Mofiz draws. A voice agent that renders live Mermaid diagrams as you describe them, in English or Bangla.
situation: Describing a system is easy; drawing it is the slow part. Diagram tools make you stop thinking and place boxes.
task: Describe a system out loud and watch the diagram appear.
Work in Bangla as naturally as in English.
result: Deployed and working, built in a single day. Voice in, diagram out, with barge-in and SVG export.
contributions: Locked the model, prompt and voice into the token — direct audio latency, but the client can't alter the persona.
Fed Mermaid parse errors back to the model so it self-corrects, capped at three tries.
Found a Windows bug: tooling injects invisible BOM characters into the API key, which HTTP headers reject.

keyResults.metricNote: 12 commits — voice, diagrams, canvas and export.

items.name: 3 retries
items.label: Self-correcting diagrams
items.note: The model fixes its own syntax error, not the user.

items.name: 2 languages
items.label: English and Bangla
items.note: Switches with the speaker, not with a setting.

title: RepoMap
summary: Point it at a GitHub repo or a local folder and get architecture diagrams, module notes and a generated README.
situation: Understanding an unfamiliar codebase is archaeology — and whoever needs the map most has just joined.
task: Turn a repo into a readable map of architecture and data flow.
Make it work on private code, not just public repos.
result: A working tool producing three Mermaid diagrams, module cards, onboarding steps and a README — cached after the first look.
contributions: Analysed local folders entirely in the browser, so private code never leaves the machine.
Chose the model on quota, not benchmarks: 500 requests a day against 20. For a free tool, that is the product.
Wrote a Mermaid repair pass that runs only after a render fails, so it can't break a diagram that worked.

keyResults.metricLabel: Tests over the parts that break
keyResults.metricNote: One fixture is the real diagram that failed on a public repo.

items.name: 500 vs 20
items.label: Requests per day
items.note: A free tool that dies after 20 uses isn't a tool.

title: Git Insight
summary: Feed it a GitHub username and it returns a card-based developer assessment written for recruiters.
situation: A GitHub profile is a wall of repo names. Anyone judging a developer from it is hoping a shape emerges.
task: Turn a username into archetype, stack, strengths and role fit.
Accept a username or a full profile URL.
result: A working tool returning a seven-card assessment, built across three bursts and deployed on Vercel.
contributions: Moved both API keys behind serverless functions — the original design would have shipped my Gemini key to every visitor.
Cut to the top 30 repos, seven fields each, and aggregated languages server-side to keep the token bill sane.
Constrained the model with a JSON contract, then hedged anyway against it not complying.

keyResults.metricNote: Sending a whole profile is neither affordable nor better.

items.name: Keys off the client
items.label: The refactor that mattered
items.note: Vite inlines prefixed env vars into the bundle.

title: Socratic AI Tutor
summary: The proof of concept for ilm AI — a tutor that refuses to just answer, asking guiding questions until the student gets there themselves.
situation: Students use AI to finish assignments without learning. ilm's bet was a tutor that refuses to answer — which only works if the refusal holds.
task: Prove the Socratic line survives a student pushing for the answer.
Show it working by voice, not just text.
result: A working proof of concept — Django REST on Gemini, React front end, full voice loop by speech or text.
contributions: Wrote the constraint into the prompt: an answer only after two or three hints, and only for general knowledge.
Used the browser's own speech APIs both ways, so voice costs nothing and needs no realtime audio service.
Kept history in a ref — the recognition callback closes over the first render, wiping memory on spoken turns.

keyResults.metricLabel: Hints before an answer is allowed
keyResults.metricNote: One paragraph of prompt is where the teaching lives.

items.name: $0 voice
items.label: Web Speech API
items.note: No audio service, no per-minute billing, nothing to deploy.

title: Otsukare
summary: A Japan-focused freelance platform connecting global IT talent with Japanese firms through AI-driven matching.

title: Fabric QR Web App
summary: Shares fabric details — images, GSM, type, references — over QR codes, killing a repetitive workflow and its errors.
